title: Fix for Fix resolving default/nondefault type parameters (#7171)
body:
The explicit type Holder#(W#(int)) and the implicit default Holder#() now correctly resolve to the same specialization Holder_, so $cast / direct assignment between them works as expected.

I will be updating the testcase to get rid of warnings (or an expect).  The existing testcase still has two warnings which I believe are testcase artifacts.

You can't keep $cast without warnings because the fix makes both types identical — so $cast is true (CASTCONST) and !$cast(...) is a 32 to 1 bit truncation (WIDTHTRUNC).

```
%Warning-CASTCONST: t_class_default_type_param.v:20:10: $cast will always return zero as 'class{}Holder__Tz1' is not castable from 'class{}Holder__'
                                                      : ... note: In instance 't'
-node: CASTDYNAMIC (HB) <e1965#> {e20ak} @dt=(HG)@(G/swu32/1)
   20 |     if (!$cast(exp, imp)) begin
      |          ^~~~~
                    ... For warning description see https://verilator.org/warn/CASTCONST?v=5.047
                    ... Use "/* verilator lint_off CASTCONST */" and lint_on around source to disable this message.
%Warning-WIDTHTRUNC: t_class_default_type_param.v:20:9: Logical operator LOGNOT expects 1 bit on the LHS, but LHS's CONST '32'h0' generates 32 bits.
                                                      : ... note: In instance 't'
-node: LOGNOT (FB) <e1281> {e20aj} @dt=(GB)@(G/w1)
   20 |     if (!$cast(exp, imp)) begin
      |         ^
                     ... For warning description see https://verilator.org/warn/WIDTHTRUNC?v=5.047
                     ... Use "/* verilator lint_off WIDTHTRUNC */" and lint_on around source to disable this message.
```
